title: Career Agency, Success Criteria, and Search Constraints · Claim Audit: Constraint Inventory
summary: A graduate is told to apply everywhere immediately, but has not defined acceptable work, financial runway, access needs, location, schedule, authorization, values, safety, or a stop rule. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the constraint inventory increment in learner-owned career decision charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.
Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Constraint Inventory · Theory Constraint Inventory
steps.instruction:
theory case — A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The theory variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Constraint Inventory contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Constraint Inventory · Theory Constraint Inventory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily. For Predict Constraint Inventory · Theory Constraint Inventory, keep Constraint Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The theory variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Constraint Inventory condition decided your prediction.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Every personal constraint must be explained to an employer to be respected. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Constraint Inventory.

theory case — A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. content.text: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The worked example variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily. A defensible solution applies Constraint Inventory, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to learner-owned career decision charter.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-5
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every personal constraint must be explained to an employer to be respected. This fails the Constraint Inventory contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: NACE 2025 Career Readiness Quick Poll (Published 2025-09-30; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Provides bounded contemporary context for skills-based graduate hiring and institutional competency integration without turning a survey into a universal employer claim.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.

steps.title: Defend the Constraint Inventory model · Theory Constraint Inventory
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Constraint Inventory: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.

Evidence target: Defend the Constraint Inventory model · Theory Constraint Inventory.

For Defend the Constraint Inventory model · Theory Constraint Inventory, keep Constraint Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.
